title: Chicken Biryani
time: Prep Time: 30 minutes | Marination Time: 2–4 hours (overnight ideal) | Cooking Time: 60 minutes

ingredients.title: For the Chicken Marinade
items: 800 g chicken, bone-in, cut into medium pieces

ingredients.title: For the Rice
items: 330 g aged basmati rice (soaked 30 min, drained)
½ tsp shahi jeera (black cumin seeds)

ingredients.title: For the Biryani Assembly
items: Few strands saffron (soaked in the warm milk)
Extra fried onions, mint & coriander for layering

instruction.title: 1. Marinate the Chicken
steps: In a large bowl, mix all marinade ingredients until smooth.
Coat chicken pieces thoroughly.
Cover and refrigerate at least 2 hours, preferably overnight.

instruction.title: 2. Prepare Fried Onions
steps: Heat oil in a wide pan, fry sliced onions until golden brown and crisp.
Drain on paper towels. Reserve some for garnish and some for marinade (already included).

instruction.title: 3. Cook the Chicken Masala
steps: Heat ghee + oil in a heavy-bottom biryani pot (or Dutch oven).
Add marinated chicken and cook on medium-high until sealed (about 5 min).
Lower heat, cover, and cook 10–12 min until chicken is 70% done (gravy thickens).
Remove from heat – keep chicken in the pot.

instruction.title: 4. Parboil the Rice
steps: Boil water with whole spices & salt.
Add soaked rice and cook until 70% cooked (grain should still have a firm core).
Drain immediately to stop cooking.

instruction.title: 5. Layer the Biryani
steps: In the same pot with chicken at the base:
Sprinkle fried onions, mint, and coriander over chicken.
Layer parboiled rice gently on top.
Drizzle saffron milk, extra ghee, and a few drops of rose water or kewra essence (optional, for restaurant aroma).
Seal the lid with foil or dough for dum (steam lock).

instruction.title: 6. Dum Cooking
steps: Stovetop: Place pot over low flame, put a heavy skillet/tawa underneath to diffuse heat, cook 25–30 min.
Oven: Preheat to 180°C (356°F), bake covered for 25 min.

instruction.title: 7. Rest & Serve
steps: Let biryani rest 10 minutes before opening.
Gently fluff from the sides – don’t mix too aggressively.
Serve with raita, salad, or mirchi ka salan.

chefTips: Rice Quality: Always use aged basmati for long, fluffy grains.
Layering Aroma: The final drizzle of saffron milk + kewra water gives that signature “royal” fragrance.
Onions: Uniformly golden fried onions make a massive flavor difference.
Marination: Overnight marination allows deeper spice penetration and tender meat.
Heat Control: Low and slow dum ensures rice absorbs flavor without overcooking.
